sloka_1145.meaning: the state of Brahma is reached

sloka_1141.meaning: O best of the Bharat dynasty
as born of the combination of the field and its knower

sloka_1122.meaning: O descendant of the Bharat dynasty
as kṣetrajña, the knower of the field

sloka_1146.meaning: nor is affected by any action
and being transcendental to the material modes

sloka_1124.meaning: That knowledge of kṣetra and kṣetrajña
and by the aphorisms of Vedānta

sloka_1126.meaning: the quality of desirelessness for honour
detachment from the objects of the senses
of birth, death, infirmity, and disease
and the vision of the goal of true knowledge

sloka_1127.meaning: and beyond non-being, beyond the material cause
beyond being, beyond the material effect

sloka_1136.meaning: Attachment to the modes (as body, senses, etc.)
Situated within (the body which is an effect of) material nature
births in higher and lower species

sloka_1135.meaning: of the state of being an enjoyer or a sufferer
via the presiding demigods of the senses, of the production of the effect, as the body, and of the cause, as the senses
Material nature (with the presence of puruṣ)

sloka_1149.meaning: the way of liberation of the living beings from material nature
between the field and its knower

sloka_1125.meaning: the ten senses (the five senses of action—voice, hands, legs, anus, and genital; and the five senses of perception – eye, tongue, skin, nose, and ear)
corresponding objects of the senses (form, taste, touch, smell, and sound)

sloka_1129.meaning: (He is) the Lord of all qualities
(Although) transcendental to the modes of nature
(He) illuminates all the senses and their functions

sloka_1134.meaning: and the transformations of the modes of nature, as happiness, unhappiness, sorrow, and delusion, etc
and the jīvātma, individual soul, described as ‘knower of the field’
the transformations – body, senses, and their functions

sloka_1133.meaning: and the object of knowledge (the Lord in His aspects of Brahma, Paramātmā, and Bhagavān)

sloka_1120.meaning: and the knower of the field

sloka_1121.meaning: as kṣetrajña, the knower of the field

sloka_1147.meaning: is not tainted by anything, is untouched

sloka_1140.meaning: when their faith is deepened by hearing
